title: Cyber Vision Administration Audit Trail
app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905)
spl:
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:health" log_type="audit"
| stats count as events by user, action, appliance_id
| sort -events
| table user, action, appliance_id, events
description: All administrative actions in OT security platforms — user logins, configuration changes, sensor management, database operations — are logged. Maintaining an audit trail of who did what and when supports regulatory compliance (IEC 62443, NERC CIP) and insider threat detection.
value: All administrative actions in OT security platforms — user logins, configuration changes, sensor management, database operations — are logged. Maintaining an audit trail of who did what and when supports regulatory compliance (IEC 62443, NERC CIP) and insider threat detection.
implementation: Both platforms provide administrative audit trails. Forward all administration events to Splunk. Build compliance reports showing all administrative actions. Alert on high-severity admin events (system reboot, database restore, sensor deletion). Track user login patterns for anomaly detection.
visualization: Admin activity timeline; user action summary table; login pattern analysis; compliance audit report.
knownFalsePositives: Administration audit events from platform upgrades, support logins, and scheduled health checks.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:health`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Both platforms provide administrative audit trails. Forward all administration events to Splunk. Build compliance reports showing all administrative actions. Alert on high-severity admin events (system reboot, database restore, sensor deletion). Track user login patterns for anomaly detection.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nozomi sourcetype="nozomi:health" log_type="audit"
| stats count as events by user, action, appliance_id
| sort -events
| table user, action, appliance_id, events
```

Understanding this SPL

**Cyber Vision Administration Audit Trail** — All administrative actions in OT security platforms — user logins, configuration changes, sensor management, database operations — are logged. Maintaining an audit trail of who did what and when supports regulatory compliance (IEC 62443, NERC CIP) and insider threat detection.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=cisco:cybervision:syslog` · Nozomi: `sourcetype=nozomi:health`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Cisco Cyber Vision Splunk Add-On` (Splunkbase 5748) · `Nozomi Networks Universal Add-on` (Splunkbase 6905). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nozomi; **sourcetype**: nozomi:health. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nozomi, sourcetype="nozomi:health". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by user, action, appliance_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Cyber Vision Administration Audit Trail**): table user, action, appliance_id, events

Step 3 — Validate
Run the search for a time range that should include both routine and change activity, then compare the same assets, flows, and alerts in the Cisco Cyber Vision UI and in Nozomi Guardian or CMC, matching whichever source the event came from. Reconcile severities, counts, and key fields in both tools.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Admin activity timeline; user action summary table; login pattern analysis; compliance audit report.
grandmaExplanation: We keep an audit of administration on our OT security platform so who changed what and when is not argued from memory.
